name: Stacked Vertical with Karaoke Highlight Captions
description: BIZDRIVE-style vertical talking-head video: top frame is screen recording, bottom is face on a circle, with optional B-roll inserts in the top frame. Same stacked layout as Template 01 — the only difference is the caption system: BIZDRIVE Karaoke (caption-highlight), a coloured box that sweeps word-by-word in sync with the speech (red box for normal words, gold box for brand / number / tech tokens).

layout.captionPosition: in the band below the bottom circle, bottom: 360px (build-highlight-captions.py arg 4)

captions.fontSize: 70px, auto-fit down if a group overflows
captions.mechanic: a coloured box sweeps word-by-word (scaleX 0->1, transform-origin left) in sync with speech; the spoken word pops scale 1.0 -> 1.07 then settles

colors.text: #ffffff — flips to #1c1206 (dark) while a gold box is active, for WCAG contrast

captions.goldTokenRules: numbers, monetary figures, durations, brand/tech names, headline terms — these get the gold box
captions.perWordTiming: interpolated inside each group by character weight (caption-groups.json carries group start/end + token text+gold only)

audio.polishChain: highpass80, lowpass12k, afftdn, agate, acompressor, loudnorm 2-pass, alimiter, apad

bgm.mixNote: mix BGM with ffmpeg amix normalize=0 so the voice keeps its polished level

broll.aiGenNote: B-roll fills the top frame (16:9-ish), not the whole screen — generate clips at 720p, 16:9, generate_audio false.

sfx.pacing: ~1 SFX per 12s of final video; fewer for clips under 60s
sfx.note: Every clip gets SFX but kept sparse — at most 5, each context-matched to the beat it lands on. mix-sfx.py enforces the rule and writes output/finals/final.mp4.

pipeline.differenceFromTemplate01: Identical stacked layout (top screen frame + bottom face circle), motion, audio, B-roll and pipeline. The only change is Step 11: build captions with scripts/build-highlight-captions.py (karaoke box sweep) instead of build-burst-captions.py (particle-burst). The caption-groups.json schema is the same — both caption systems consume it.

reference.job: (none yet — first job will become the reference)
